entries.notes: Known modeling bug benchmark: Algorand-style vote certification with intentionally weak certificate threshold allows conflicting vote certificates to coexist, exposing a safety counterexample.

Bug seed: Casper FFG-style finality gadget with conflicting justification paths; the supermajority link rule is intentionally weakened so that two incompatible checkpoints can both be justified, violating agreement.

DiemBFT epoch-change kernel modeling threshold-signature LedgerQC formation under partial synchrony with n>3t Byzantine resilience. Agreement holds because the 2t+1 distinct vote threshold prevents conflicting QCs from forming in the same epoch.
Bug seed: DLS-style partial-synchrony protocol where the transition from asynchronous to synchronous mode admits a split-decision trace; processes can commit to different values before GST arrives.

Bug seed: GRANDPA-style finality voting rule intentionally weakened; the prevote/precommit quorum interaction allows two conflicting blocks to both reach finality, violating the accountable safety property.

HoneyBadgerBFT-style asynchronous common subset kernel with two-stage RBC+BA protocol under omission faults (n=3f+1). Agreement holds because each stage requires n-f messages ensuring at least one honest quorum overlap.

Bug seed: chained HotStuff model with intentionally weakened commit rule; the three-chain safety argument fails because the guard on the third QC does not enforce view monotonicity, admitting conflicting commit paths.

Crypto-object decisive bug seed: HighQC is modeled without conflict admissibility under full equivocation. A Byzantine signer can create conflicting certificate variants (value=true/false), leading to split decisions and an agreement violation.

Crypto-object decisive faithful HotStuff kernel: HighQC uses signer-scoped certificate formation with `conflicts exclusive` and no equivocation, preventing conflicting certificates and preserving agreement.

Faithful-network HotStuff-style safety kernel with identity-selective delivery, signed authentication, and per-recipient fault injection. Uses distinct sender counting in receive guards to model realistic quorum certificate formation.

Jolteon/Fast-HotStuff-style protocol kernel under partial synchrony with n>3t Byzantine resilience. Models HighQC certificate formation from 2t+1 NewView messages; agreement holds via quorum intersection of the fast-path certificate threshold.

Multi-Paxos single-round kernel with crash fault model (n>3t). Models slot-based agreement via 2t+1 Learn-message quorum; safety holds because any two quorums of size 2t+1 overlap in at least one honest process.

Bug seed: Narwhal/Bullshark DAG-based certificate rule intentionally allows conflicting delivery decisions; the certificate formation threshold is set below the quorum intersection bound required for agreement.

Classic single-decree Paxos kernel with crash fault model (n>3t). Models the core promise-accept-learn pattern via 2t+1 Learn-message quorum; agreement follows from the majority quorum intersection property.

Bug seed: core PBFT pre-prepare/prepare/commit flow with intentionally under-constrained quorum threshold; the prepare certificate does not require 2f+1 distinct senders, allowing Byzantine nodes to forge a conflicting certificate.

Crypto-object decisive bug seed for PBFT-style prepare certificates: with full equivocation and no certificate conflict policy, Byzantine traffic can produce conflicting PrepareQC variants and violate agreement.

Crypto-object decisive faithful PBFT kernel: PrepareQC formation is signer-scoped and `conflicts exclusive` under no-equivocation assumptions, preventing contradictory certificates and preserving agreement.

PBFT-shaped liveness regression sentinel for unbounded proving: no commit source is modeled, so fair non-terminating cycles remain reachable under weak fairness. This entry anchors expected non-live outcomes for bounded and unbounded liveness checks.
PBFT-shaped nontrivial CI anchor with partial-synchrony/adversary/message structure and a deterministic live target. Used for stable unbounded fair-liveness certificate generation and replay gates.

Minimal PBFT safety kernel without view-change: models the pre-prepare/prepare/commit three-phase pattern with correct 2t+1 quorum thresholds under n>3t Byzantine resilience. Proved safe by k-induction at k=8.

Faithful-network PBFT safety kernel with identity-selective delivery, signed channels, and per-recipient fault injection. Mirrors the minimal PBFT kernel but with distinct sender counting and explicit equivocation=none enforcement.

Bug seed: PBFT with view-change extension where the carry-over of prepared certificates across views is intentionally incomplete; a new primary can propose a value conflicting with a previously prepared one.

QBFT round-change kernel modeling the Istanbul BFT variant with n=3f+1 Byzantine resilience. The preprepare-prepare-roundchange flow ensures agreement because the 2f+1 distinct Prepare quorum prevents conflicting pre-prepared values across rounds.

Raft election safety kernel under omission faults with partial synchrony (n>3t). Models single-term leader election via 2t+1 Vote quorum; agreement holds because each replica votes at most once per term, ensuring at most one leader per term.

Bug seed: Bracha reliable-broadcast variant with intentionally weakened quorum thresholds in the Echo/Ready phases; permits two honest processes to accept conflicting values because the quorum intersection is insufficient.

Bracha reliable-broadcast safety kernel with correct n>3t threshold: Init/Echo/Ready three-phase pattern where 2t+1 Echo threshold and 2t+1 Ready threshold ensure quorum intersection guarantees agreement. Proved safe by k-induction at k=8.

Faithful-network Bracha reliable-broadcast with identity-selective delivery, signed authentication, and distinct sender counting. Mirrors the minimal kernel but models per-recipient fault injection and explicit no-equivocation enforcement.

Bug seed: SBFT-style committee-based protocol with intentionally weak committee selection and commit conditions; the fast-path commit threshold allows a Byzantine-dominated committee to finalize conflicting values.

Bug seed: Streamlet notarization rule intentionally allows conflicting finalized outcomes; the three-consecutive-notarized-block finality rule is weakened so that two conflicting chains can both satisfy the finalization criterion.

Temporal leads-to violation benchmark for bounded liveness: models a protocol where the liveness property (eventually all processes decide) can be violated by a stalling execution, but no fair cycle exists up to the exploration depth.

Bug seed: Tendermint locking/precommit conditions intentionally relaxed; the lock-change rule does not properly enforce that a process must see a polka for a different value before unlocking, admitting conflicting precommits.
Crypto-object decisive bug seed for Tendermint polka certificates: full equivocation plus missing conflict admissibility allows conflicting Polka variants and split decisions.

Crypto-object decisive faithful Tendermint kernel: signer-scoped Polka formation with `conflicts exclusive` under no-equivocation assumptions eliminates conflicting certificates and preserves agreement.

Minimal sanity benchmark that exercises all five check modes (verify, prove, liveness, fair_liveness, prove_fair). Trivially satisfies all properties to ensure the engine pipeline is end-to-end functional.

Tusk DAG-based certificate protocol kernel under Byzantine faults (n>3t) with signed authentication. Models certificate formation from 2t+1 distinct Vote messages; agreement follows from the quorum intersection property of the DAG layer.

Viewstamped Replication safety kernel with crash fault model (n=2f+1). Three-phase StartView/Prepare/Commit flow where each stage requires n-f messages; agreement holds because any two quorums of n-f crash-tolerant replicas overlap.

Bug seed: Viewstamped Replication variant with zero-threshold transitions that bypass the quorum guards; processes can skip directly to the commit phase without collecting sufficient StartView/Prepare acknowledgments.

Faithful-network Viewstamped Replication with identity-selective delivery, signed authentication, distinct sender counting, and per-recipient fault injection. Mirrors the minimal VR kernel under a more detailed network model.

Zab-style atomic broadcast kernel under omission faults with partial synchrony (n=2f+1). Three-phase Proposal/Ack/Commit flow where each stage requires n-f messages; agreement holds because omission-tolerant quorums overlap.

Bug seed: Zab-style atomic broadcast with zero-threshold transitions that bypass quorum guards; replicas can advance through Proposal/Ack/Commit phases without sufficient acknowledgments, admitting conflicting commits.

Faithful-network Zab atomic broadcast with identity-selective delivery, signed authentication, distinct sender counting, and per-recipient omission fault injection. Mirrors the minimal Zab kernel under a more detailed network model.
Bug seed: Zyzzyva fast-path certificate threshold intentionally set below 3f+1, allowing a Byzantine coalition to forge a conflicting fast-path certificate and violate agreement.
Reliable Broadcast with liveness property (termination). Safety (agreement) holds; bounded fair-liveness may report spurious cycle due to counter abstraction over-approximation.
Reliable Broadcast with liveness bug: self-loop in echoed phase allows non-termination under weak fairness. Safety (agreement) still holds since the bug is a liveness bug, not a safety bug.
Example demonstrating enum type declarations in the DSL. Simple majority-vote protocol with enum Vote { yes, no } phases.
Reliable Broadcast with cohort-selective network semantics. Demonstrates per-cohort Byzantine delivery channels with higher-fidelity counter abstraction.
Reliable Broadcast with process-selective network semantics. Demonstrates concrete process identities with per-process channels for instance-exact verification.
RBFT/QBFT consensus safety model with distinct-sender Prepare/Commit quorums (2f+1 of 3f+1). Agreement holds because quorum overlap ensures locked value carries across rounds.
Minimmit 2-round finality BFT (n>=5f+1) safety model. Dual-quorum structure: L-notarisation (n-f) for finalization, M-notarisation (2f+1) for view change. Agreement via quorum overlap.
Phoenixx linear BFT with endorser sampling. HotStuff-inspired 3-phase with NQC (2f+1) and probabilistic EQC via committee. Safety from quorum overlap plus honest endorser guarantee.
Reliable broadcast with mid-execution reconfiguration (tightens threshold after delivery). Agreement trivially holds since only one decision value exists in single-value broadcast.

Reconfiguration bug: threshold weakening via reconfigure{t=t-1} breaks quorum overlap. One process decides true via strong quorum, another reconfigures and decides false via weak guard. Exercises epoch-aware SMT encoding.

Clock/timeout bug: process that receives Reject ticks clock and aborts via timeout guard, while another process collects enough Votes and decides true. Exercises clock variable encoding and timeout guard constraints.

FIFO channel bug: weak guard (1 Vote) allows conflicting decisions. Process can decide true on any Vote or false on any Reject. Exercises FIFO queue head/tail encoding alongside standard agreement checking.

DAG round bug: despite dag_round structure with parent dependencies, weak guards (1 message) allow conflicting true/false decisions. Exercises DAG activation variable encoding alongside agreement property checking.
Clock/timeout safe counterpart: timeout only triggers retry and eventual quorum-based true decision. Exercises clock ticks and timeout guards without introducing conflicting terminal values.
Enum unsafe counterpart: weak thresholds and abort path allow conflicting enum decisions (yes/no), providing the missing unsafe pair for enum-valued local state.
Bounded-log unsafe counterpart: append operations are exercised on conflicting decision paths with weak guards, producing an agreement violation sentinel for bounded collection workflows.
Committee unsafe counterpart: certificate threshold is reduced to b+1, allowing conflicting committee outcomes and violating agreement.
Safe message_args example: filtered distinct-sender quorum guards for Vote(round=0,value=*) under signed/no-equivocation assumptions preserve agreement.
Unsafe message_args example: weak filtered guards (received >= 1) on Vote(round=0,value=*) allow immediate conflicting terminal decisions.
Leader-role unsafe counterpart: single leader can equivocate across proposal values, and validators using weak accept rules can decide conflicting values.
Pacemaker unsafe counterpart: under-constrained view progression plus weak commit thresholds permits conflicting decisions across the same logical view workflow.
Temporal liveness anchor for []<> progress: the making_progress flag starts true and never changes, so every bounded path already satisfies the always-eventually obligation.
Temporal liveness bug seed for []<> progress: the making_progress flag is permanently false in the only phase, so the bounded liveness checker finds a non-live counterexample immediately.
Temporal leads-to safe anchor: once active is true, delivered is also already true in the steady phase, so the response obligation is satisfied on every reachable execution prefix.
Temporal leads-to bug seed: active is permanently true while delivered is permanently false in the stalled phase, yielding an immediate bounded non-liveness witness.
Nested temporal liveness safe anchor: all three phase-completion flags start true, so the chained leads-to/eventually obligation closes without requiring any additional transitions.
Nested temporal liveness bug seed: the third completion flag is permanently false, so the multi-stage eventuality cannot be satisfied and bounded liveness reports a counterexample.
Two-role liveness anchor: the proposer's proposed flag and the replica's acknowledged flag both start true, so the cross-role response obligation holds on every reachable state.

Reconfiguration gap reproducer: strict mode now rejects the resilience-weakening update before verification. The manifest records an inconclusive placeholder until cert-suite supports expected validation-error outcomes.

Reconfiguration multi-update reproducer: strict mode now rejects conflicting parameter rewrites before BMC runs. The manifest uses an inconclusive placeholder because validation errors are not yet first-class cert-suite outcomes.
